# Phase 10 Stress Testing Plan

Phase: 10.0.33 - Constitutional Civilization Failure Simulation, Stress Testing & Catastrophic Survivability Doctrine  
Status: Planning-only. Runtime remains disabled.

## Objective

Define how constitutional civilization can model catastrophic stress, hostile pressure, collapse risk, degradation, and survivability without creating destructive authority or runtime execution.

## Scope

- Constitutional stress-testing doctrine.
- Civilization failure simulation.
- Catastrophic survivability philosophy.
- Anti-collapse governance standards.
- Institutional stress survivability.
- Controlled failure modeling.
- Resilience validation.
- Hostile-pressure survivability.
- Existential-risk simulation.
- Constitutional degradation detection.
- Survivability verification.
- Continuity under catastrophe.

## Non-Scope

- Runtime enablement.
- APIs, providers, adapters, credentials, or OAuth.
- Prisma, Supabase, Twilio, email, or background jobs.
- Autonomous stress-testing systems.
- Automatic catastrophic simulation systems.
- Self-modifying survivability systems.
- Destructive git operations.
- Repository mutation systems.
- Rollback execution.
- Deployment authority.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Planning docs remain isolated under governance documentation paths.
- All simulations are explicitly hypothetical and non-destructive.
- Stress testing is fail-closed and human-reviewed.
- Resilience validation does not create automatic authority.
- Continuity under catastrophe preserves evidence, dissent, memory, identity, and restoration legitimacy.
- Runtime and deployment authority remain prohibited.
